revision.text: #redirect[[en:Minegazuka_Kofun]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''峯ヶ塚古墳'''（みねがづかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里2丁目に存在する古墳時代後期の[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]の南西部に位置する。国指定[[史跡]]。
== 概要 == 峯ヶ塚古墳は、墳丘の長さ96メートル、前方部の幅74.4メートル・高さ10.5メートル、後円部は直径56メートル・高さ9メートルの前方後円墳である。
江戸時代には、[[日本武尊]]白鳥陵に比定されていた（現在は、[[軽里大塚古墳]]を比定）。また、[[允恭天皇]]皇子の[[木梨軽皇子]]の墓との伝承もあった。
墳丘は二段に築かれており、二段目斜面の裾部分のみに数段の角礫が葺いてあるほかは、表面に葺石はみられない。 1992年の発掘調査で、後円部墳頂中央部の盗掘壙(とうくつこう)を掘り進めた結果、竪穴式石室が現れた。 石室や盗掘壙から、大刀・鉄鏃などの武器、挂甲小札(けいこうこぎね)などの武具、轡(くつわ)・鐙(あぶみ)などの馬具、装身具、玉類などが大量に出土した。また、成人男性の骨や歯なども発見されている。 出土した埴輪や横穴式石室の特徴から、6世紀初頃(古墳時代後期)の築造と考えられている。
また、発掘調査により、峯ヶ塚古墳は二重濠をもつ古墳であることが判明した。内濠の幅約11メートル、内堤の幅約18メートル、外濠の幅約8メートル、墓域は総長約168メートル、幅約148メートルの大規模なものである。古墳時代後期には古墳が小さくなるなかで、二重濠を持つ古墳を築造できた被葬者は大王級の権力のある人物であったと推測される。
1974年に国の史跡に指定されている。
また、1987年に峯ヶ塚古墳を含む5.3ヘクタールが地区公園として都市計画が決定され、羽曳野市により1998年度を目指して公園と専門資料館の整備が検討されていたが、2006年現在完成していない。
== 発掘調査 == *1987年～ 羽曳野市教育委員会 **1987年6月15日～8月4日 外濠の調査（第1次調査） **1989年12月18日～12月25日 遺構現状調査と地通レーダー探査（第2次調査） **1991年2月1日～3月30日 内濠の調査（第3次調査） **1991年10月21日～1992年4月29日 後円部の調査（第4次調査） **（その後も、調査が行われている。）
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.mapion.co.jp/c/f?uc=1&grp=all&nl=34/32/55.965&el=135/36/00.156&scl=25000&bid=Mlink 地図（mapionより）] *[http://maps.google.com/maps?ie=UTF8&om=0&z=18&ll=34.552395,135.59728&spn=0.002063,0.003026&t=k google.mapsの衛星画像]
[[category:古墳|みねがづか]] [[category:大阪府の史跡|みねがづかこふん]]

== 概要 == 峯ヶ塚古墳は、墳丘の長さ96メートル、前方部の幅74.4メートル・高さ10.5メートル、後円部は直径56メートル・高さ9メートルの前方後円墳である。

[[1974年]]（昭和49年）に国の史跡に指定されている。
また、[[1987年]]（昭和62年）に峯ヶ塚古墳を含む5.3ヘクタールが地区公園として都市計画が決定され、羽曳野市により[[1998年]]（平成10年）度を目指して公園と専門資料館の整備が検討されていたが、[[2006年]]（平成18年）現在完成していない。
== 発掘調査 == *1987年～ 羽曳野市教育委員会 **1987年6月15日～8月4日 外濠の調査（第1次調査） **1989年12月18日～12月25日 遺構現状調査と地通レーダー探査（第2次調査） **1991年2月1日～3月30日 内濠の調査（第3次調査） **1991年10月21日～1992年4月29日 後円部の調査（第4次調査） **（その後も、調査が行われている。）

また、発掘調査により、峯ヶ塚古墳は二重濠をもつ古墳であることが判明した。内濠の幅約11メートル、内堤の幅約18メートル、外濠の幅約8メートル、墓域は総長約168メートル、幅約148メートルの大規模なものである。古墳時代後期には古墳が全般的に縮小傾向をみせるなかで、二重濠を持つ古墳を築造できた被葬者は[[大王 (ヤマト王権)|大王]]級の権力のある人物であったと推測される。
[[1974年]]（昭和49年）に国の史跡に指定されている。
また、[[1987年]]（昭和62年）に峯ヶ塚古墳を含む5.3ヘクタールが地区公園として都市計画が決定され、羽曳野市により[[1998年]]（平成10年）度を目指して公園と専門資料館の整備が検討されていたが、[[2006年]]（平成18年）現在完成していない。
== 発掘調査 == *1987年～ 羽曳野市教育委員会 **1987年6月15日～8月4日 外濠の調査（第1次調査） **1989年12月18日～12月25日 遺構現状調査と地通レーダー探査（第2次調査） **1991年2月1日～3月30日 内濠の調査（第3次調査） **1991年10月21日～1992年4月29日 後円部の調査（第4次調査） **（その後も、調査が行われている。）
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.mapion.co.jp/c/f?uc=1&grp=all&nl=34/32/55.965&el=135/36/00.156&scl=25000&bid=Mlink 地図（mapionより）] *[http://maps.google.com/maps?ie=UTF8&om=0&z=18&ll=34.552395,135.59728&spn=0.002063,0.003026&t=k google.mapsの衛星画像]

{{日本の古墳| 名称=峯ヶ塚古墳| 所在地=大阪府羽曳野市軽里2丁目| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|8.0|N|135|35|49.8|E|region:JP-27}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 築造年代=6世紀初頭| 規模=全長96m、高さ10.5m| 被葬者=| 史跡指定=[[1974年]]国指定| 出土品=武器、武具、馬具、装身具、玉類、埴輪、男性人骨| 画像=[[Image:Minagadukakohun.jpg|250px]]|}} '''峯ヶ塚古墳'''（みねがづかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里2丁目に存在する[[古墳時代]]後期の[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]の南西部に位置する。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。

[[江戸時代]]には、[[日本武尊]]白鳥陵に比定されていた（現在は、[[軽里大塚古墳]]を比定）。また、[[允恭天皇]]皇子の[[木梨軽皇子]]の墓との[[伝承]]もあった。

また、発掘調査により、峯ヶ塚古墳は二重濠をもつ古墳であることが判明した。内濠の幅約11メートル、内堤の幅約18メートル、外濠の幅約8メートル、墓域は総長約168メートル、幅約148メートルの大規模なものである。古墳時代後期には古墳が全般的に縮小傾向をみせるなかで、二重濠を持つ古墳を築造できた被葬者は[[大王 (ヤマト王権)|大王]]級の権力のある人物であったと推測される。
[[1974年]]（昭和49年）に国の史跡に指定されている。
また、[[1987年]]（昭和62年）に峯ヶ塚古墳を含む5.3ヘクタールが地区公園として都市計画が決定され、羽曳野市により[[1998年]]（平成10年）度を目指して公園と専門資料館の整備が検討されていたが、[[2006年]]（平成18年）現在完成していない。
== 発掘調査 == *1987年～ 羽曳野市教育委員会 **1987年6月15日～8月4日 外濠の調査（第1次調査） **1989年12月18日～12月25日 遺構現状調査と地通レーダー探査（第2次調査） **1991年2月1日～3月30日 内濠の調査（第3次調査） **1991年10月21日～1992年4月29日 後円部の調査（第4次調査） **（その後も、調査が行われている。）

{{日本の古墳| 名称=峯ヶ塚古墳| 所属=[[古市古墳群]]| 所在地=大阪府羽曳野市軽里2丁目| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|8.0|N|135|35|49.8|E|region:JP-27}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 築造年代=6世紀初頭| 規模=全長96m、高さ10.5m| 被葬者=| 指定文化財=[[1974年]]国指定| 出土品=武器、武具、馬具、装身具、玉類、埴輪、男性人骨| 画像=[[Image:Minagadukakohun.jpg|250px]]|}} '''峯ヶ塚古墳'''（みねがづかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里2丁目に存在する[[古墳時代]]後期の[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]の南西部に位置する。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
== 概要 == 峯ヶ塚古墳は、墳丘の長さ96メートル、前方部の幅74.4メートル・高さ10.5メートル、後円部は直径56メートル・高さ9メートルの前方後円墳である。
[[江戸時代]]には、[[日本武尊]]白鳥陵に比定されていた（現在は、[[軽里大塚古墳]]を比定）。また、[[允恭天皇]]皇子の[[木梨軽皇子]]の墓との[[伝承]]もあった。
墳丘は二段に築かれており、二段目斜面の裾部分のみに数段の角礫が葺いてあるほかは、表面に[[葺石]]はみられない。 [[1992年]]（平成4年）の[[発掘調査]]で、後円部墳頂中央部の盗掘壙（とうくつこう）を掘り進めた結果、[[竪穴式石室]]が現れた。 石室や盗掘壙から、[[大刀]]・[[鉄鏃]]などの武器、挂甲小札（けいこうこざね）などの[[武具]]、轡（くつわ）・[[鐙]]（あぶみ）などの[[馬具]]、装身具、[[玉]]類などが大量に出土した。また、成人[[男性]]の[[骨]]や[[歯]]なども出土している。 出土した[[埴輪]]や横穴式石室の特徴から、[[6世紀]]初頃（古墳時代後期）の築造と考えられている。
また、発掘調査により、峯ヶ塚古墳は二重濠をもつ古墳であることが判明した。内濠の幅約11メートル、内堤の幅約18メートル、外濠の幅約8メートル、墓域は総長約168メートル、幅約148メートルの大規模なものである。古墳時代後期には古墳が全般的に縮小傾向をみせるなかで、二重濠を持つ古墳を築造できた被葬者は[[大王 (ヤマト王権)|大王]]級の権力のある人物であったと推測される。
[[1974年]]（昭和49年）に国の史跡に指定されている。
また、[[1987年]]（昭和62年）に峯ヶ塚古墳を含む5.3ヘクタールが地区公園として都市計画が決定され、羽曳野市により[[1998年]]（平成10年）度を目指して公園と専門資料館の整備が検討されていたが、[[2006年]]（平成18年）現在完成していない。
== 発掘調査 == *1987年～ 羽曳野市教育委員会 **1987年6月15日～8月4日 外濠の調査（第1次調査） **1989年12月18日～12月25日 遺構現状調査と地通レーダー探査（第2次調査） **1991年2月1日～3月30日 内濠の調査（第3次調査） **1991年10月21日～1992年4月29日 後円部の調査（第4次調査） **（その後も、調査が行われている。）
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.mapion.co.jp/c/f?uc=1&grp=all&nl=34/32/55.965&el=135/36/00.156&scl=25000&bid=Mlink 地図（mapionより）] *[http://maps.google.com/maps?ie=UTF8&om=0&z=18&ll=34.552395,135.59728&spn=0.002063,0.003026&t=k google.mapsの衛星画像]
{{DEFAULTSORT:みねかつかこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:古市古墳群]]

[[江戸時代]]には、[[日本武尊]]白鳥陵に比定されていた（現在は、[[軽里大塚古墳]]を比定）。また、[[允恭天皇]]皇子の[[木梨軽皇子]]の墓との[[伝承]]もあった。
墳丘は二段に築かれており、二段目斜面の裾部分のみに数段の角礫が葺いてあるほかは、表面に[[葺石]]はみられない。 [[1992年]]（平成4年）の[[発掘調査]]で、後円部墳頂中央部の盗掘壙（とうくつこう）を掘り進めた結果、[[竪穴式石室]]が現れた。 石室や盗掘壙から、[[大刀]]・[[鉄鏃]]などの武器、挂甲小札（けいこうこざね）などの[[武具]]、轡（くつわ）・[[鐙]]（あぶみ）などの[[馬具]]、装身具、[[玉]]類などが大量に出土した。また、成人[[男性]]の[[骨]]や[[歯]]なども出土している。 出土した[[埴輪]]や[[横穴式石室]]の特徴から、[[6世紀]]初頃（古墳時代後期）の築造と考えられている。
また、発掘調査により、峯ヶ塚古墳は二重濠をもつ古墳であることが判明した。内濠の幅約11メートル、内堤の幅約18メートル、外濠の幅約8メートル、墓域は総長約168メートル、幅約148メートルの大規模なものである。古墳時代後期には古墳が全般的に縮小傾向をみせるなかで、二重濠を持つ古墳を築造できた被葬者は[[大王 (ヤマト王権)|大王]]級の権力のある人物であったと推測される。
[[1974年]]（昭和49年）に国の史跡に指定されている。
また、[[1987年]]（昭和62年）に峯ヶ塚古墳を含む5.3[[ヘクタール]]が地区公園として都市計画が決定され、羽曳野市により[[1998年]]（平成10年）度を目指して公園と専門資料館の整備が検討されていたが、[[2006年]]（平成18年）現在完成していない。
== 発掘調査 == *1987年～ 羽曳野市教育委員会 **1987年6月15日～8月4日 外濠の調査（第1次調査） **1989年12月18日～12月25日 遺構現状調査と地通レーダー探査（第2次調査） **1991年2月1日～3月30日 内濠の調査（第3次調査） **1991年10月21日～1992年4月29日 後円部の調査（第4次調査） **（その後も、調査が行われている。）

墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部）|}} '''峯ヶ塚古墳'''（みねがづかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里2丁目にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]（[[文化遺産 (世界遺産)|世界文化遺産]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。 == 概要 == 墳丘の長さ96[[メートル]]、前方部の幅74.4メートル・高さ10.5メートル、後円部は直径56メートル・高さ9メートルの前方後円墳である。 [[江戸時代]]には、[[日本武尊]]白鳥陵に比定されていた（現在は、[[軽里大塚古墳]]を比定）。また、[[允恭天皇]]皇子の[[木梨軽皇子]]の墓との[[伝承]]もあった。 墳丘は二段に築かれており、二段目斜面の裾部分のみに数段の角礫が葺いてあるほかは、表面に[[葺石]]はみられない。 [[1992年]]（平成4年）の[[発掘調査]]で、後円部墳頂中央部の盗掘壙（とうくつこう）を掘り進めた結果、[[竪穴式石室]]が現れた。 石室や盗掘壙から、[[大刀]]・[[鉄鏃]]などの武器、挂甲小札（けいこうこざね）などの[[武具]]、轡（くつわ）・[[鐙]]（あぶみ）などの[[馬具]]、装身具、[[玉]]類などが大量に出土した。また、成人[[男性]]の[[骨]]や[[歯]]なども出土している。 出土した[[埴輪]]や[[横穴式石室]]の特徴から、[[6世紀]]初頃（古墳時代後期）の築造と考えられている。 また、発掘調査により、峯ヶ塚古墳は二重濠をもつ古墳であることが判明した。内濠の幅約11メートル、内堤の幅約18メートル、外濠の幅約8メートル、墓域は総長約168メートル、幅約148メートルの大規模なものである。古墳時代後期には古墳が全般的に縮小傾向をみせるなかで、二重濠を持つ古墳を築造できた被葬者は[[大王 (ヤマト王権)|大王]]級の権力のある人物であったと推測される。 [[1974年]]（昭和49年）に国の史跡に指定されている。 また、[[1987年]]（昭和62年）に峯ヶ塚古墳を含む5.3[[ヘクタール]]が地区公園として都市計画が決定され、羽曳野市により[[1998年]]（平成10年）度を目指して公園と専門資料館の整備が検討されていたが、[[2006年]]（平成18年）現在完成していない。 == 発掘調査 == *1987年～　羽曳野市教育委員会 **1987年6月15日～8月4日　外濠の調査（第1次調査） **1989年12月18日～12月25日　遺構現状調査と地通レーダー探査（第2次調査） **1991年2月1日～3月30日　内濠の調査（第3次調査） **1991年10月21日～1992年4月29日　後円部の調査（第4次調査） **（その後も、調査が行われている。） ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html 峯ヶ塚古墳] - 羽曳野市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:みねかつかこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:古市古墳群]]

(ヤマト王権)|大王]]級の権力のある人物であったと推測される。[[1974年]]（昭和49年）に国の史跡に指定された。 また[[1987年]]（昭和62年）には、峯ヶ塚古墳を含む5.3[[ヘクタール]]が地区公園として都市計画が決定され、現在は「峰塚公園」として整備されている{{Cite '''峯ヶ塚古墳'''（みねがづかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里2丁目にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]（[[文化遺産 (世界遺産)|世界文化遺産]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている{{Cite web|author=羽曳野市教育委員会事務局|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=国指定/登録文化財|work=羽曳野市|accessdate=2021-11-22}}。 == 概要 == 墳丘の長さ96[[メートル]]、前方部の幅74.4メートル・高さ10.5メートル、後円部は直径56メートル・高さ9メートルの前方後円墳である。 [[江戸時代]]には、[[日本武尊]]白鳥陵に比定されていた（現在は、[[軽里大塚古墳]]を比定）。また、[[允恭天皇]]皇子の[[木梨軽皇子]]の墓との[[伝承]]もあった。 墳丘は二段に築かれており、二段目斜面の裾部分のみに数段の角礫が葺いてあるほかは、表面に[[葺石]]はみられない。 [[1992年]]（平成4年）の[[発掘調査]]で、後円部墳頂中央部の盗掘壙（とうくつこう）を掘り進めた結果、[[竪穴式石室]]が現れた。 石室や盗掘壙から、[[大刀]]・[[鉄鏃]]などの武器、挂甲小札（けいこうこざね）などの[[武具]]、轡（くつわ）・[[鐙]]（あぶみ）などの[[馬具]]、装身具、[[玉]]類などが大量に出土した。また、成人[[男性]]の[[骨]]や[[歯]]なども出土している。 出土した[[埴輪]]や[[横穴式石室]]の特徴から、[[6世紀]]初頃（古墳時代後期）の築造と考えられている。 また、発掘調査により、峯ヶ塚古墳は二重濠をもつ古墳であることが判明した。内濠の幅約11メートル、内堤の幅約18メートル、外濠の幅約8メートル、墓域は総長約168メートル、幅約148メートルの大規模なものである。古墳時代後期には古墳が全般的に縮小傾向をみせるなかで、二重濠を持つ古墳を築造できた被葬者は[[大王 (ヤマト王権)|大王]]級の権力のある人物であったと推測される。[[1974年]]（昭和49年）に国の史跡に指定された。 また[[1987年]]（昭和62年）には、峯ヶ塚古墳を含む5.3[[ヘクタール]]が地区公園として都市計画が決定され、現在は「峰塚公園」として整備されている{{Cite web|author=羽曳野市土木部道路公園課|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=峰塚公園について|work=羽曳野市|accessdate=2021-11-22}}。 == 発掘調査 == *1987年～　羽曳野市教育委員会 **1987年6月15日～8月4日　外濠の調査（第1次調査） **1989年12月18日～12月25日　遺構現状調査と地通レーダー探査（第2次調査） **1991年2月1日～3月30日　内濠の調査（第3次調査） **1991年10月21日～1992年4月29日　後円部の調査（第4次調査） **（その後も、調査が行われている。） ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html 峯ヶ塚古墳] - 羽曳野市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:みねかつかこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:古市古墳群]]

(ヤマト王権)|大王]]級の権力のある人物であったと推測される。[[1974年]]（昭和49年）に国の史跡に指定された。 また[[1987年]]（昭和62年）には、峯ヶ塚古墳を含む5.3[[ヘクタール]]が地区公園として都市計画が決定され、現在は「峰塚公園」として整備されている{{Cite web|author=羽曳野市土木部道路公園課|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=峰塚公園について|work=羽曳野市|accessdate=2021-11-22}}。 == 発掘調査 == *1987年～　羽曳野市教育委員会 **1987年6月15日～8月4日　外濠の調査（第1次調査） **1989年12月18日～12月25日　遺構現状調査と地通レーダー探査（第2次調査） **1991年2月1日～3月30日　内濠の調査（第3次調査） **1991年10月21日～1992年4月29日　後円部の調査（第4次調査） **（その後も、調査が行われている。） ==アクセス== ===電車・バス=== * {{近鉄南大阪線古市駅}}から 西に1.1km 徒歩約15分 / 西出口近鉄バス2駅4分-徒歩1分（最寄りバス停「軽里一丁目」へは羽曳野線81.82.85.87系統に乗車） * {{近鉄南大阪線藤井寺駅}}から 南に2.6km 徒歩35分 / 南出口近鉄バス5駅8分-徒歩6分 （最寄りバス停「羽曳山住宅」へは羽曳野線60.61.62.66.78系統に乗車） ===車=== 国道170号線「軽里北」曲がってすぐ。隣接の峰塚公園に駐車場有り ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html 峯ヶ塚古墳] - 羽曳野市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:みねかつかこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] web|author=羽曳野市土木部道路公園課|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=峰塚公園について|work=羽曳野市|accessdate=2021-11-22}}。 == 発掘調査 == *1987年～　羽曳野市教育委員会 **1987年6月15日～8月4日　外濠の調査（第1次調査） **1989年12月18日～12月25日　遺構現状調査と地通レーダー探査（第2次調査） **1991年2月1日～3月30日　内濠の調査（第3次調査） **1991年10月21日～1992年4月29日　後円部の調査（第4次調査） **（その後も、調査が行われている。） ==アクセス== ===電車・バス=== * 近鉄南大阪線[[古市駅 (大阪府)]]から 西に1.1km 徒歩約15分 / 近鉄バス2駅4分-徒歩1分（最寄りのバス停「軽里一丁目」へは、西出口から羽曳野線81.82.85.87系統に乗車） * 近鉄南大阪線[[藤井寺駅]]から 南に2.6km 徒歩約35分 / 近鉄バス5駅8分-徒歩6分 （最寄りのバス停「羽曳山住宅」へは、南出口から羽曳野線60.61.62.66.78系統に乗車） ===車=== 国道170号線「軽里北」交差点曲がってすぐ。隣接の「峰塚公園」に駐車場有り ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html 峯ヶ塚古墳] - 羽曳野市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:みねかつかこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:古市古墳群]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruses||奈良県天理市にある古墳|峯塚古墳}} {{日本の古墳 |名称=峯ヶ塚古墳 |所属=[[古市古墳群]] |所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里2丁目（峰塚公園内） |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |築造時期=6世紀初頭 |規模=全長96[[メートル|m]]、高さ10.5m |被葬者= |史跡=[[1974年]]（昭和49年）国指定 |出土品=[[武器]]、武具、[[馬具]]、装身具、玉類、[[埴輪]]、男性人骨 |画像=[[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=峯ヶ塚古墳|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=前方後円墳-西南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=大阪府内内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.33|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''峯ヶ塚古墳'''（みねがづかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里2丁目にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]（[[文化遺産 (世界遺産)|世界文化遺産]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている{{Cite web|和書|author=羽曳野市教育委員会事務局|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=国指定/登録文化財|work=羽曳野市|accessdate=2021-11-22}}。 == 概要 == 墳丘の長さ96[[メートル]]、前方部の幅74.4メートル・高さ10.5メートル、後円部は直径56メートル・高さ9メートルの前方後円墳である。 [[江戸時代]]には、[[日本武尊]]白鳥陵に比定されていた（現在は、[[軽里大塚古墳]]を比定）。また、[[允恭天皇]]皇子の[[木梨軽皇子]]の墓との[[伝承]]もあった。 墳丘は二段に築かれており、二段目斜面の裾部分のみに数段の角礫が葺いてあるほかは、表面に[[葺石]]はみられない。 [[1992年]]（平成4年）の[[発掘調査]]で、後円部墳頂中央部の盗掘壙（とうくつこう）を掘り進めた結果、[[竪穴式石室]]が現れた。 石室や盗掘壙から、[[大刀]]・[[鉄鏃]]などの武器、挂甲小札（けいこうこざね）などの[[武具]]、轡（くつわ）・[[鐙]]（あぶみ）などの[[馬具]]、装身具、[[玉]]類などが大量に出土した。また、成人[[男性]]の[[骨]]や[[歯]]なども出土している。 出土した[[埴輪]]や[[横穴式石室]]の特徴から、[[6世紀]]初頃（古墳時代後期）の築造と考えられている。 また、発掘調査により、峯ヶ塚古墳は二重濠をもつ古墳であることが判明した。内濠の幅約11メートル、内堤の幅約18メートル、外濠の幅約8メートル、墓域は総長約168メートル、幅約148メートルの大規模なものである。古墳時代後期には古墳が全般的に縮小傾向をみせるなかで、二重濠を持つ古墳を築造できた被葬者は[[大王 (ヤマト王権)|大王]]級の権力のある人物であったと推測される。[[1974年]]（昭和49年）に国の史跡に指定された。 また[[1987年]]（昭和62年）には、峯ヶ塚古墳を含む5.3[[ヘクタール]]が地区公園として都市計画が決定され、現在は「峰塚公園」として整備されている{{Cite web|和書|author=羽曳野市土木部道路公園課|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=峰塚公園について|work=羽曳野市|accessdate=2021-11-22}}。 == 発掘調査 == *1987年～　羽曳野市教育委員会 **1987年6月15日～8月4日　外濠の調査（第1次調査） **1989年12月18日～12月25日　遺構現状調査と地通レーダー探査（第2次調査） **1991年2月1日～3月30日　内濠の調査（第3次調査） **1991年10月21日～1992年4月29日　後円部の調査（第4次調査） **（その後も、調査が行われている。） ==アクセス== ===電車・バス=== * 近鉄南大阪線[[古市駅 (大阪府)]]から 西に1.1km 徒歩約15分 / 近鉄バス2駅4分-徒歩1分（最寄りのバス停「軽里一丁目」へは、西出口から羽曳野線81.82.85.87系統に乗車） * 近鉄南大阪線[[藤井寺駅]]から 南に2.6km 徒歩約35分 / 近鉄バス5駅8分-徒歩6分 （最寄りのバス停「羽曳山住宅」へは、南出口から羽曳野線60.61.62.66.78系統に乗車） ===車=== 国道170号線「軽里北」交差点曲がってすぐ。隣接の「峰塚公園」に駐車場有り ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html 峯ヶ塚古墳] - 羽曳野市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:みねかつかこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:古市古墳群]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox Ancient Tomb |Name = Minegazuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |Coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Dimensions = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Burial Figure = |Designation = National Historic Site (1974) |Artifacts = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, male skeletal remains |Image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Minegazuka Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the supposed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a designation now applied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of Burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous artifacts, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === Construction Period === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand scale suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Minegazuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were designated as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: * June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) * December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) * February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) * October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) * Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: * Walk: Approx. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) * Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: * Walk: Approx. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) * Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]]

It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]]

{{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox Ancient Tomb |Name = Minegazuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |Coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Dimensions = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Burial Figure = |Designation = National Historic Site (1974) |Artifacts = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, male skeletal remains |Image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Minegazuka Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the supposed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a designation now applied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of Burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous artifacts, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === Construction Period === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand scale suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Minegazuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were designated as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: * June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) * December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) * February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) * October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) * Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: * Walk: Approx. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) * Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: * Walk: Approx. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) * Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |Name = Minegazuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |Coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Dimensions = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Burial Figure = |Designation = National Historic Site (1974) |Artifacts = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, male skeletal remains |Image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Minegazuka Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the supposed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a designation now applied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of Burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous artifacts, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === Construction Period === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand scale suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Minegazuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were designated as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: * June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) * December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) * February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) * October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) * Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: * Walk: Approx. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) * Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: * Walk: Approx. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) * Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]]峯ヶ塚古墳]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |Name = Minegazuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |Coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Dimensions = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Burial Figure = |Designation = National Historic Site (1974) |Artifacts = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, male skeletal remains |Image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Minegazuka Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the supposed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a designation now applied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of Burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous artifacts, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === Construction Period === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand scale suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Minegazuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were designated as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: * June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) * December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) * February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) * October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) * Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: * Walk: Approx. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) * Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: * Walk: Approx. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) * Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:kk]][[ja: [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:kk]][[ja: [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja: [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:kk]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:kk]][[ja: 峯ヶ塚古墳]]

Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of Burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous artifacts, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === Construction Period === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand scale suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Minegazuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were designated as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: * June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) * December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) * February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) * October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) * Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: * Walk: Approx. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) * Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: * Walk: Approx. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) * Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - {{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |Name = Minegazuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |Coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Dimensions = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Burial Figure = |Designation = National Historic Site (1974) |Artifacts = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, male skeletal remains |Image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Minegazuka Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the supposed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a designation now applied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of Burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous artifacts, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === Construction Period === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand scale suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Minegazuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were designated as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: * June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) * December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) * February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) * October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) * Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: * Walk: Approx. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) * Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: * Walk: Approx. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) * Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:[[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:[[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:kk]][[ja:[[Category:kk]] [[Category:test if the jawiki is actually present]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]]

{{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |Name = Minegazuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |Coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Dimensions = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Burial Figure = |Designation = National Historic Site (1974) |Artifacts = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, male skeletal remains |image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Minegazuka Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the supposed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a designation now applied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of Burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous artifacts, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === Construction Period === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand scale suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Minegazuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were designated as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ** June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ** December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ** February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ** October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ** Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ** Walk: Approx. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ** Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ** Walk: Approx. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ** Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:test if the jawiki is actually present]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]]

{{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |Name = Minegazuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |Coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Dimensions = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Burial Figure = |Designation = National Historic Site (1974) |Artifacts = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, male skeletal remains |image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Minegazuka Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the supposed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a designation now applied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of Burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous artifacts, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === Construction Period === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand scale suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Minegazuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were designated as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: Approx. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: Approx. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Minegazuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]]

The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: Approx. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: Approx. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. {{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |Name = Minegazuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |Coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Dimensions = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Burial Figure = |Designation = National Historic Site (1974) |Artifacts = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, male skeletal remains |image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Minegazuka Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the supposed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a designation now applied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of Burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous artifacts, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === Construction Period === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand scale suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Minegazuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were designated as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: Approx. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: Approx. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Numerous artifacts, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === Construction Period === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand scale suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Minegazuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were designated as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: Approx. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: Approx. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Minegazuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]]

{{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |Name = Minegazuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |Coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Dimensions = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Burial Figure = |Designation = National Historic Site (1974) |Artifacts = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, male skeletal remains |image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Minegazuka Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the supposed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a designation now applied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of Burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous artifacts, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === Construction Period === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand scale suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Minegazuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were designated as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: Approx. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: Approx. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Minegazuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]]
