Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Minegazuka Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated {{ill|World other_designation|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated other_designation|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a designation now a11lied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of Burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous artifacts, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Minegazuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were designated as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Minegazuka Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated {{ill|World other_designation|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated other_designation|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a designation now a11lied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of Burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous artifacts, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Minegazuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were designated as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Minegazuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |area = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Burial Figure = |Designation = National other_designation (1974) |burial goods = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, male skeletal remains |image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Minegazuka Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated {{ill|World other_designation|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The kofun is listed as a National other_designation in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated other_designation|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a designation now a11lied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of Burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous artifacts, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Minegazuka Kofun was designated a National other_designation in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were designated as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Minegazuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |area = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Burial Figure = |Designation = National other_designation (1974) |burial goods = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, male skeletal remains |image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Minegazuka Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designation1 {{ill|World other_designation|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The kofun is listed as a National other_designation in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated other_designation|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a designation now a11lied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of Burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Minegazuka Kofun was designation1 a National other_designation in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were designation1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Minegazuka Kofun was designation1 a National other_designation in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were designation1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Minegazuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |area = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Burial Figure = |Designation = National other_other_designation (1974) |burial goods = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, male skeletal remains |image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Minegazuka Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a other_designation1 {{ill|World other_other_designation|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The kofun is listed as a National other_other_designation in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated other_other_designation|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a other_designation now a11lied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of Burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Minegazuka Kofun was other_designation1 a National other_other_designation in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were other_designation1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Minegazuka Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a other_designation1 {{ill|World other_other_designation|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The kofun is listed as a National other_other_designation in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated other_other_designation|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a other_designation now a11lied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of Burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Minegazuka Kofun was other_designation1 a National other_other_designation in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were other_designation1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Minegazuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |area = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Burial Figure = |Designation = National other_designation (1974) |burial goods = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, male skeletal remains |image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Minegazuka Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a other_designation1 {{ill|World other_designation|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The kofun is listed as a National other_designation in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated other_designation|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a other_designation now a11lied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of Burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Minegazuka Kofun was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were other_designation1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Minegazuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |area = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Burial Figure = |Designation = National other_designation (1974) |burial goods = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, male skeletal remains |image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Minegazuka Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a other_designation1 {{ill|World other_designation|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The kofun is listed as a National other_designation in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated other_designation|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a other_designation now a11lied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of Burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Minegazuka Kofun was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were other_designation1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * Minegazuka Kofun was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were other_designation1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Minegazuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |area = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Interred_individual = |Designation = National other_designation (1974) |burial goods = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, male skeletal remains |image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Minegazuka Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a other_designation1 {{ill|World other_designation|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The kofun is listed as a National other_designation in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated other_designation|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a other_designation now a11lied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Minegazuka Kofun was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were other_designation1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Minegazuka Kofun was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were other_designation1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Minegazuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |area = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Interred_individual = |Designation = National other_designation (1974) |burial goods = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, male skeletal remains |image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Minegazuka Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a other_designation1 {{ill|World other_designation|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The kofun is listed as a National other_designation in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated other_designation|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a other_designation now a11lied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Minegazuka Kofun was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were other_designation1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Minegazuka Kofun was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were other_designation1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} *
