This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Minegazuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} |area = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Interred_individual = |designation1 = National Historic Site (1974) |burial goods = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, male skeletal remains |image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Minegazuka Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 {{ill|World Historic Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)|12=draft}}. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Historic Site|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a Historic Site now a11lied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳|12=draft}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子|12=draft}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|12=draft}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀|12=draft}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. Minegazuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were Historic Site1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅|12=draft}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pp error]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]] [[sn:inegazuka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Minegazuka Kofun}}

Minegazuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were Historic Site1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅|12=draft}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]] [[sn:inegazuka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Minegazuka Kofun}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Minegazuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} |area = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Interred_individual = |designation1 = National Historic Site (1974) |burial goods = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, male skeletal remains |image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Minegazuka Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 {{ill|World Historic Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)|12=draft}}. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Historic Site|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a Historic Site now a11lied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳|12=draft}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子|12=draft}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|12=draft}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀|12=draft}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. Minegazuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were Historic Site1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅|12=draft}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]] [[sn:inegazuka Kofun]] {{citation needed|Minegazuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀|12=draft}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. Minegazuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were Historic Site1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅|12=draft}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]] [[sn:inegazuka Kofun]] {{citation needed|Minegazuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Minegazuka Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 {{ill|World Historic Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)|12=draft}}. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Historic Site|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a Historic Site now a11lied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳|12=draft}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子|12=draft}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|12=draft}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀|12=draft}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. Minegazuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were Historic Site1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅|12=draft}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]] [[sn:inegazuka Kofun]] {{citation needed|Minegazuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a Historic Site now a11lied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳|12=draft}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子|12=draft}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|12=draft}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀|12=draft}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. Minegazuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were Historic Site1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅|12=draft}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]]{{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]] [[sn:inegazuka Kofun]] {{citation needed|Minegazuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Minegazuka Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 {{ill|World Historic Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)|12=draft}}. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Historic Site|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子|12=draft}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|12=draft}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀|12=draft}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. Minegazuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were Historic Site1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅|12=draft}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Minegazuka Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 {{ill|World Historic Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)|12=draft|12=draft}}. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Historic Site|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a Historic Site now a11lied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子|12=draft|12=draft}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀|12=draft|12=draft}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft|12=draft}}. Minegazuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were Historic Site1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Minegazuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]] [[sn:inegazuka Kofun]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Minegazuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |area = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Interred_individual = |designation1 = National Historic Site (1974) |burial goods = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, male skeletal remains |image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Minegazuka Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 {{ill|World Historic Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)|12=draft|12=draft}}. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Historic Site|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a Historic Site now a11lied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子|12=draft|12=draft}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀|12=draft|12=draft}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft|12=draft}}. Minegazuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were Historic Site1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Minegazuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]] [[sn:inegazuka Kofun]]

Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Minegazuka Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. revision.comment: 23 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:峯ヶ塚古墳 up to rev 98039554
revision.text:
{{Otheruses||奈良県天理市にある古墳|峯塚古墳}} {{日本の古墳 |名称=峯ヶ塚古墳 |所属=[[古市古墳群]] |所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里2丁目（峰塚公園内） |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |築造時期=6世紀初頭 |規模=全長96[[メートル|m]]、高さ10.5m |被葬者= |史跡=[[1974年]]（昭和49年）国指定 |出土品=[[武器]]、武具、[[馬具]]、装身具、玉類、[[埴輪]]、男性人骨 |画像=[[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=峯ヶ塚古墳|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=前方後円墳-西南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=大阪府内内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.33|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''峯ヶ塚古墳'''（みねがづかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里2丁目にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]（[[文化遺産 (世界遺産)|世界文化遺産]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている{{Cite web|和書|author=羽曳野市教育委員会事務局|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=国指定/登録文化財|work=羽曳野市|accessdate=2021-11-22}}。 == 概要 == 墳丘の長さ96[[メートル]]、前方部の幅74.4メートル・高さ10.5メートル、後円部は直径56メートル・高さ9メートルの前方後円墳である。 [[江戸時代]]には、[[日本武尊]]白鳥陵に比定されていた（現在は、[[軽里大塚古墳]]を比定）。また、[[允恭天皇]]皇子の[[木梨軽皇子]]の墓との[[伝承]]もあった。 墳丘は二段に築かれており、二段目斜面の裾部分のみに数段の角礫が葺いてあるほかは、表面に[[葺石]]はみられない。 [[1992年]]（平成4年）の[[発掘調査]]で、後円部墳頂中央部の盗掘壙（とうくつこう）を掘り進めた結果、[[竪穴式石室]]が現れた。 石室や盗掘壙から、[[大刀]]・[[鉄鏃]]などの武器、挂甲小札（けいこうこざね）などの[[武具]]、轡（くつわ）・[[鐙]]（あぶみ）などの[[馬具]]、装身具、[[玉]]類などが大量に出土した。また、成人[[男性]]の[[骨]]や[[歯]]なども出土している。 出土した[[埴輪]]や[[横穴式石室]]の特徴から、[[6世紀]]初頃（古墳時代後期）の築造と考えられている。 また、発掘調査により、峯ヶ塚古墳は二重濠をもつ古墳であることが判明した。内濠の幅約11メートル、内堤の幅約18メートル、外濠の幅約8メートル、墓域は総長約168メートル、幅約148メートルの大規模なものである。古墳時代後期には古墳が全般的に縮小傾向をみせるなかで、二重濠を持つ古墳を築造できた被葬者は[[大王 (ヤマト王権)|大王]]級の権力のある人物であったと推測される。[[1974年]]（昭和49年）に国の史跡に指定された。 また[[1987年]]（昭和62年）には、峯ヶ塚古墳を含む5.3[[ヘクタール]]が地区公園として都市計画が決定され、現在は「峰塚公園」として整備されている{{Cite web|和書|author=羽曳野市土木部道路公園課|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=峰塚公園について|work=羽曳野市|accessdate=2021-11-22}}。 == 発掘調査 == *1987年～　羽曳野市教育委員会 **1987年6月15日～8月4日　外濠の調査（第1次調査） **1989年12月18日～12月25日　遺構現状調査と地通レーダー探査（第2次調査） **1991年2月1日～3月30日　内濠の調査（第3次調査） **1991年10月21日～1992年4月29日　後円部の調査（第4次調査） **（その後も、調査が行われている。） ==アクセス== ===電車・バス=== * 近鉄南大阪線[[古市駅 (大阪府)]]から 西に1.1km 徒歩約15分 / 近鉄バス2駅4分-徒歩1分（最寄りのバス停「軽里一丁目」へは、西出口から羽曳野線81.82.85.87系統に乗車） * 近鉄南大阪線[[藤井寺駅]]から 南に2.6km 徒歩約35分 / 近鉄バス5駅8分-徒歩6分 （最寄りのバス停「羽曳山住宅」へは、南出口から羽曳野線60.61.62.66.78系統に乗車） ===車=== 国道170号線「軽里北」交差点曲がってすぐ。隣接の「峰塚公園」に駐車場有り ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html 峯ヶ塚古墳] - 羽曳野市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:みねかつかこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:古市古墳群]]

{{Otheruses||奈良県天理市にある古墳|峯塚古墳}} {{日本の古墳 |名称=峯ヶ塚古墳 |所属=[[古市古墳群]] |所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里2丁目（峰塚公園内） |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |築造時期=6世紀初頭 |規模=全長96[[メートル|m]]、高さ10.5m |被葬者= |史跡=[[1974年]]（昭和49年）国指定 |出土品=[[武器]]、武具、[[馬具]]、装身具、玉類、[[埴輪]]、男性人骨 |画像=[[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=峯ヶ塚古墳|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=前方後円墳-西南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=大阪府内内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.33|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''峯ヶ塚古墳'''（みねがづかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里2丁目にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]（[[文化遺産 (世界遺産)|世界文化遺産]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている{{Cite web|和書|author=羽曳野市教育委員会事務局|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=国指定/登録文化財|work=羽曳野市|accessdate=2021-11-22}}。 == 概要 == 墳丘の長さ96[[メートル]]、前方部の幅74.4メートル・高さ10.5メートル、後円部は直径56メートル・高さ9メートルの前方後円墳である。 [[江戸時代]]には、[[日本武尊]]白鳥陵に比定されていた（現在は、[[軽里大塚古墳]]を比定）。また、[[允恭天皇]]皇子の[[木梨軽皇子]]の墓との[[伝承]]もあった。 墳丘は二段に築かれており、二段目斜面の裾部分のみに数段の角礫が葺いてあるほかは、表面に[[葺石]]はみられない。 [[1992年]]（平成4年）の[[発掘調査]]で、後円部墳頂中央部の盗掘壙（とうくつこう）を掘り進めた結果、[[竪穴式石室]]が現れた。 石室や盗掘壙から、[[大刀]]・[[鉄鏃]]などの武器、挂甲小札（けいこうこざね）などの[[武具]]、轡（くつわ）・[[鐙]]（あぶみ）などの[[馬具]]、装身具、[[玉]]類などが大量に出土した。また、成人[[男性]]の[[骨]]や[[歯]]なども出土している。 出土した[[埴輪]]や[[横穴式石室]]の特徴から、[[6世紀]]初頃（古墳時代後期）の築造と考えられている。 また、発掘調査により、峯ヶ塚古墳は二重濠をもつ古墳であることが判明した。内濠の幅約11メートル、内堤の幅約18メートル、外濠の幅約8メートル、墓域は総長約168メートル、幅約148メートルの大規模なものである。古墳時代後期には古墳が全般的に縮小傾向をみせるなかで、二重濠を持つ古墳を築造できた被葬者は[[大王 (ヤマト王権)|大王]]級の権力のある人物であったと推測される。[[1974年]]（昭和49年）に国の史跡に指定された。 また[[1987年]]（昭和62年）には、峯ヶ塚古墳を含む5.3[[ヘクタール]]が地区公園として都市計画が決定され、現在は「峰塚公園」として整備されている{{Cite web|和書|author=羽曳野市土木部道路公園課|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=峰塚公園について|work=羽曳野市|accessdate=2021-11-22}}。 == 発掘調査 == *1987年～　羽曳野市教育委員会 **1987年6月15日～8月4日　外濠の調査（第1次調査） **1989年12月18日～12月25日　遺構現状調査と地通レーダー探査（第2次調査） **1991年2月1日～3月30日　内濠の調査（第3次調査） **1991年10月21日～1992年4月29日　後円部の調査（第4次調査） **（その後も、調査が行われている。） ==アクセス== ===電車・バス=== * 近鉄南大阪線[[古市駅 (大阪府)]]から 西に1.1km 徒歩約15分 / 近鉄バス2駅4分-徒歩1分（最寄りのバス停「軽里一丁目」へは、西出口から羽曳野線81.82.85.87系統に乗車） * 近鉄南大阪線[[藤井寺駅]]から 南に2.6km 徒歩約35分 / 近鉄バス5駅8分-徒歩6分 （最寄りのバス停「羽曳山住宅」へは、南出口から羽曳野線60.61.62.66.78系統に乗車） ===車=== 国道170号線「軽里北」交差点曲がってすぐ。隣接の「峰塚公園」に駐車場有り ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html 峯ヶ塚古墳] - 羽曳野市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:みねかつかこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:古市古墳群]]

Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Minegazuka Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a Historic Site1 {{ill|World Historic Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Historic Site|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a Historic Site now a11lied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Minegazuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were Historic Site1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Minegazuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]] [[sn:inegazuka Kofun]] {{translated page|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|version=98039554|comment=Imported full ja history}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Minegazuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} |area = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Interred_individual = |designation1 = National Historic Site (1974) |burial goods = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, male skeletal remains |image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Minegazuka Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 {{ill|World Historic Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)|12=draft}}. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Historic Site|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a Historic Site now a11lied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳|12=draft}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子|12=draft}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|12=draft}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀|12=draft}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. Minegazuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were Historic Site1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅|12=draft}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website {{citation needed|Minegazuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|version=98039554|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]] [[sn:inegazuka Kofun]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Minegazuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} |built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}} |area = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Interred_individual = |designation1 = National Historic Site (1974) |burial goods = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}}, male skeletal remains |image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Minegazuka Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}, a Historic Site1 {{ill|World Historic Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/World Historic Site|qq=}}. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Historic Site|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a Historic Site now a11lied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kinashi no Karu|qq=}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ingyō|qq=}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese swords|qq=}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|qq=}}. Minegazuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were Historic Site1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Station (Osaka)|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|qq=}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Station|qq=}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|version=98039554|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]] [[sn:inegazuka Kofun]]
