Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Minegazuka Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}, a Historic Site1 {{ill|World Historic Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/World Historic Site|qq=}}. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Historic Site|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a Historic Site now a11lied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kinashi no Karu|qq=}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ingyō|qq=}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese swords|qq=}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|qq=}}. Minegazuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were Historic Site1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Station (Osaka)|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|qq=}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Station|qq=}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|version=98039554|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]] [[sn:inegazuka Kofun]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Minegazuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} |built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}} |area = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Interred_individual = |designation1 = National Historic Site (1974) |burial goods = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}}, male skeletal remains |image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Minegazuka Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}, a Historic Site1 {{ill|World Historic Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/World Historic Site|qq=}}. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Historic Site|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a Historic Site now a11lied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kinashi no Karu|qq=}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ingyō|qq=}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese swords|qq=}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|qq=}}. Minegazuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were Historic Site1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Station (Osaka)|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|qq=}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Station|qq=}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|version=98039554|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]] [[sn:inegazuka Kofun]]

Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Minegazuka Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}, a Historic Site1 {{ill|World Historic Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/World Historic Site|qq=}}. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Historic Site|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a Historic Site now a11lied to the {{ill|Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kinashi no Karu|qq=}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ingyō|qq=}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese swords|qq=}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|qq=}}. Minegazuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were Historic Site1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Station (Osaka)|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|qq=}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Station|qq=}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|version=98039554|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]] [[sn:inegazuka Kofun]]

Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Minegazuka Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} that is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}, a Historic Site1 {{ill|World Historic Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/World Historic Site|qq=}}. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Historic Site|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a Historic Site now a11lied to the {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was also linked to legends about {{ill|Prince Kinashi no Karu|ja|木梨軽皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kinashi no Karu|qq=}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ingyō|qq=}}. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including {{ill|Japanese swords|ja|大刀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese swords|qq=}}, iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}} (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|qq=}}. Minegazuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were Historic Site1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Station (Osaka)|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|qq=}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From {{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Station|qq=}}: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|version=98039554|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]] [[sn:inegazuka Kofun]]

The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From [[:en:Furuichi Station (Osaka)|Furuichi Station (Osaka)]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]]: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From [[:en:Fujiidera Station|Fujidera Station]]: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|version=98039554|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]] [[sn:inegazuka Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Minegazuka Kofun | part_of = [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]] |location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |built = Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]] |area = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Interred_individual = |designation1 = National Historic Site (1974) |burial goods = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], male skeletal remains |image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Minegazuka Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] that is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]], a Historic Site1 [[:en:Cultural heritage|World Historic Site]]. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Historic Site|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a Historic Site now a11lied to the {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was also linked to legends about [[:en:Prince Kinashi no Karu|Prince Kinashi no Karu]], a son of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Ingyō]]. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including [[:en:大刀|Japanese swords]], iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]] (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]]. Minegazuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were Historic Site1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From [[:en:Furuichi Station (Osaka)|Furuichi Station (Osaka)]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]]: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From [[:en:Fujiidera Station|Fujidera Station]]: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|version=98039554|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]] [[sn:inegazuka Kofun]] [[Category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]]

The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From [[:en:Furuichi Station (Osaka)|Furuichi Station (Osaka)]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]]: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From [[:en:Fujiidera Station|Fujidera Station]]: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|version=98039554|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]] [[sn:inegazuka Kofun]] [[Category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Minegazuka Kofun | part_of = [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]] |location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |built = Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]] |area = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Interred_individual = |designation1 = National Historic Site (1974) |burial goods = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], male skeletal remains |image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Minegazuka Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] that is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]], a Historic Site1 [[:en:Cultural heritage|World Historic Site]]. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Historic Site|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a Historic Site now a11lied to the {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was also linked to legends about [[:en:Prince Kinashi no Karu|Prince Kinashi no Karu]], a son of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Ingyō]]. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including [[:en:大刀|Japanese swords]], iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]] (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]]. Minegazuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were Historic Site1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From [[:en:Furuichi Station (Osaka)|Furuichi Station (Osaka)]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]]: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From [[:en:Fujiidera Station|Fujidera Station]]: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|version=98039554|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]] [[sn:inegazuka Kofun]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]]

The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From [[:en:Furuichi Station (Osaka)|Furuichi Station (Osaka)]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]]: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From [[:en:Fujiidera Station|Fujidera Station]]: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|version=98039554|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]] [[sn:inegazuka Kofun]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture�]] {{Commons category|Minegazuka Kofun}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Minegazuka Kofun | part_of = [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]] |location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |built = Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]] |area = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Interred_individual = |designation1 = National Historic Site (1974) |burial goods = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], male skeletal remains |image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Minegazuka Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] that is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]], a Historic Site1 [[:en:Cultural heritage|World Historic Site]]. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Historic Site|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a Historic Site now a11lied to the {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was also linked to legends about [[:en:Prince Kinashi no Karu|Prince Kinashi no Karu]], a son of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Ingyō]]. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including [[:en:大刀|Japanese swords]], iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]] (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]]. Minegazuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were Historic Site1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From [[:en:Furuichi Station (Osaka)|Furuichi Station (Osaka)]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]]: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From [[:en:Fujiidera Station|Fujidera Station]]: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|version=98039554|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]] [[sn:inegazuka Kofun]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Minegazuka Kofun}}

The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From [[:en:Furuichi Station (Osaka)|Furuichi Station (Osaka)]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]]: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From [[:en:Fujiidera Station|Fujidera Station]]: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|version=98039554|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]] [[sn:inegazuka Kofun]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Minegazuka Kofun}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Nara Prefecture's Tenri City tumulus|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Minegazuka Kofun | part_of = [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]] |location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], Karisato 2-chome (within Minezuka Park) |coordinates = {{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E|region:JP-27}} |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |built = Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]] |area = Length: 96m, Height: 10.5m |Interred_individual = |designation1 = National Historic Site (1974) |burial goods = Weapons, armor, horse fittings, ornaments, beads, [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], male skeletal remains |image = [[File:Minegazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the left, rear mound in the back right) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Minegazuka Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=08.3|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=50.3|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|08.3|N|135|35|50.3|E}}}} }} '''Minegazuka Kofun''' (峯ヶ塚古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] that is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]], a Historic Site1 [[:en:Cultural heritage|World Historic Site]]. The kofun is listed as a National Historic Site in Japan{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Education Committee|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2559.html|title=Nationally Designated Historic Site|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Overview == Minegazuka Kofun has a total length of 96 meters. The front portion of the mound measures 74.4 meters in width and 10.5 meters in height, while the rear portion is 56 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. During the Edo period, this tumulus was identified as the su11osed burial site of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} (White Bird Mausoleum), a Historic Site now a11lied to the {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|軽里大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was also linked to legends about [[:en:Prince Kinashi no Karu|Prince Kinashi no Karu]], a son of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Ingyō]]. The mound consists of two tiers. Stone fragments arranged in steps are found at the base of the second-tier slopes, but no [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (stone coverings) are present on the surface. === Discovery of burial Chamber === Excavations conducted in 1992 revealed a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the center of the rear mound. Numerous burial goods, including [[:en:大刀|Japanese swords]], iron arrowheads, armor plates (keko kozane), and horse fittings such as bridles and stirrups, were uncovered. Jewelry, beads, and skeletal remains of an adult male, including teeth, were also found. === built === Based on the excavated haniwa and chamber structure, Minegazuka Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]] (late Kofun period). === Double Moats === The site features a double moat structure. The inner moat is approximately 11 meters wide, the inner embankment is about 18 meters wide, and the outer moat is 8 meters wide. The total area of the tomb precinct is about 168 meters in length and 148 meters in width. This grand map_size suggests the buried figure was likely a person of high status, possibly a king-level authority in the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]]. Minegazuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1974. === Park Development === In 1987, 5.3 hectares of land, including Minegazuka Kofun, were Historic Site1 as a district park. The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From [[:en:Furuichi Station (Osaka)|Furuichi Station (Osaka)]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]]: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From [[:en:Fujiidera Station|Fujidera Station]]: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|version=98039554|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]] [[sn:inegazuka Kofun]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Minegazuka Kofun}}

The area is now known as "Minezuka Park" and has been developed for public use{{Cite web|language=ja|author=Habikino City Road and Park Division|url=https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/doboku/dourokouen/dourokouen/6933.html|title=About Minezuka Park|work=Habikino City|accessdate=2021-11-22}}. == Excavation History == * 1987– Present, conducted by Habikino City Education Committee: ''' June 15 – August 4, 1987: Outer moat survey (1st phase) ''' December 18 – 25, 1989: Structural and ground radar surveys (2nd phase) ''' February 1 – March 30, 1991: Inner moat survey (3rd phase) ''' October 21, 1991 – April 29, 1992: Rear mound survey (4th phase) ''' Additional surveys continue to be conducted. == Access == === By Train or Bus === * From [[:en:Furuichi Station (Osaka)|Furuichi Station (Osaka)]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]]: ''' Walk: A11rox. 15 minutes west (1.1 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 81, 82, 85, or 87 (4 minutes, 2 stops) and walk 1 minute to the "Karisato 1-chome" stop. * From [[:en:Fujiidera Station|Fujidera Station]]: ''' Walk: A11rox. 35 minutes south (2.6 km) ''' Bus: Take Kintetsu Bus Line 60, 61, 62, 66, or 78 (8 minutes, 5 stops) and walk 6 minutes to the "Habikiyama Jutaku" stop. === By Car === Located near the "Karisato Kita" intersection on National Route 170. Parking is available at the adjacent "Minezuka Park." == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minegazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_chuki/2383.html Minegazuka Kofun] - Habikino City Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|version=98039554|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Minegazuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯ヶ塚古墳]] [[sn:inegazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Minegazuka Kofun}}
